data.title: Singapore tops Asia-Pacific fit-out costs at US$2,029 per sq m
data.description:
Knight Frank expects 2% to 5% rises.

Singapore is the most expensive Asia-Pacific city for office fit-outs, with average costs of US$2,029 per square metre, according to Knight Frank.

The firm said Singapore edged Tokyo at US$1,994 per square metre and Taipei at US$1,593 per square metre, based on market data from the fourth quarter of 2025.

Knight Frank attributed Singapore’s ranking to higher labour costs, constrained contractor capacity, and stringent building and sustainability standards, adding that premium fit-outs in the city-state command a large premium over the rest of Southeast Asia.

Across the region, fit-out costs are expected to rise 2% to 5% over the next 12 months as construction labour markets stay tight and companies invest more in workplace upgrades, the report said.

Knight Frank added that sustainability requirements, including green building certifications and materials compliance, are increasingly treated as baseline specifications, including in Singapore.

data.title: HDB resale volumes drop 29% as prices stay flat
data.description:
Resale index holds at 209.7 in February.

HDB resale transactions fell 29% MoM to 1,670 in February 2026, whilst overall prices were unchanged, with the resale index flat at 209.7.

Prices in Mature Estates rose 0.3% in February, whilst Non-Mature Estates fell 0.5%.

By flat type, 4-room resale prices rose 0.4%, but 3-room, 5-room, and Executive prices fell 0.8%, 0.4%, and 0.4%, respectively. Year-on-year, overall prices were up 1.5% versus February 2025.

Million-dollar resale flats slipped to 122 deals from 146 in January, making up 7.3% of transactions, with Toa Payoh recording the most such sales at 18. Bukit Merah and Queenstown followed with 17 each.

The highest-priced resale flat in February was a $1.70m 5-room unit at Skyterrace @ Dawson, whilst the top deal in Non-Mature Estates was a $1.47m 5-room unit in Punggol Drive.

data.title: Tedge retail podium relaunched at $13.88m as CBRE markets freehold strata units
data.description:
EOI closes 8 April.

CBRE is marketing a rare freehold suburban ground-floor retail podium at Tedge, 328 Changi Road, with an indicative guide price of $13.88m, or about $2,490 psf on the existing strata area.

The sale is via an expression of interest exercise that closes on 8 April at 3 pm, and covers four adjoining ground-floor retail strata units in the five-storey mixed-use development completed in 2023.

The units have a combined strata area of about 5,576 sq ft and include one F&B unit, one bakery takeaway unit, and two shop units, with the podium currently fully occupied, CBRE said.

CBRE said the development sits on a corner plot with dual road frontage spanning 75 metres at the junction of Changi Road and Telok Kurau Road, and is positioned between Eunos and Kembangan MRT stations within a short walking distance, with access to the PIE and ECP.

As a commercial property, foreigners and companies are eligible to buy, and no Additional Buyer’s Stamp Duty or Seller’s Stamp Duty applies, CBRE said.

data.title: ComfortDelGro opens Ubi automotive centre with 58 EV chargers
data.description:
Five-storey 27,400-sq-m site adds 260 bays.

ComfortDelGro Engineering has opened one of Singapore’s largest integrated automotive engineering centres at 320 Ubi Road 3 as it expands electric vehicle servicing capacity.

The five-storey facility spans more than 27,400 square metres and has over 260 vehicle bays, 58 EV charging points, and battery storage rooms.

With 43% of new car registrations in Singapore now electric, the group said the centre will support EV services, including maintenance and charging, as well as high-voltage battery and system diagnosis and repair.

The site also houses the ComfortDelGro Engineering Academy, which will provide the Land Transport Authority’s National Electric Vehicle Specialist Safety programmes for technicians.

The centre has commenced operations, with a progressive ramp-up of specialised services scheduled through the second quarter of 2026, and ComfortDelGro said members of the public and fleet partners can visit from today.

data.title: Analysts flag split sentiment in Lentor Central bids
data.description:
Top bid sets a higher benchmark.

Analysts said a GuocoLand-led consortium topped the Lentor Central residential tender with a $657.1m offer, translating to $1,278 psf ppr, setting a new high among Lentor GLS land rates since 2021.

CBRE said the premium over the second bid was about 6% at $1,208 psf ppr, whilst the lowest offer of $950 psf ppr was roughly 35% below the top bid, reflecting divergent views on pricing and risk across bidders.

Most market commentators said the site drew five bids, though Huttons described the closing as having four bidders, and noted a 5.7% gap between the top and second bids.

PropNex and Knight Frank said bidders were underpinned by the Lentor precinct’s ability to absorb supply, citing strong take-up across earlier launches and limited unsold stock, even with further pipeline still to come.

On pricing, PropNex said the land rate could translate into average selling prices above $2,500 psf, whilst Knight Frank said launch prices could start around $2,600 psf and average above $2,700 psf, with Huttons pointing to subsale prices that have exceeded $2,400 psf in the area.

data.title: Daily Markets Briefing: STI down 2.11%; Top stock is DFIRG
data.description:
DFI Retail Group is the top stock with a 3.87% increase.

The Straits Times Index (STI) closed at 4,812.750, down 2.11% on Wednesday, 4 March, at 5:25 p.m.

DFI Retail Group was the most active stock, followed by Keppel DC Reit (unchanged) and Frasers Cpt Tr (-0.44%).

Keppel Ltd. and ST Engineering were at the foot.

data.title: CLAR CEO William Tay lifts direct stake to 1.1 million units
data.description:
His holdings represent about 0.024% of the REIT’s total issued units.

CapitaLand Ascendas REIT CEO William Tay Wee Leong received 171,013 units under the REIT’s incentive schemes, increasing his direct holdings from 927,940 units to 1,098,953 units.

Following the transaction, Tay’s holdings represent about 0.024% of the REIT’s total issued units.

The units were received under the CapitaLand Ascendas REIT Management Limited Performance Unit Plan and Restricted Unit Plan.

In a separate disclosure, 145,407 awards under the Performance Unit Plan vested, with the release made partly in the form of units and partly in cash.

Following the vesting, Tay’s remaining rights and awards under the incentive schemes declined from 455,792 to 287,183.

data.title: Q & M Dental to acquire SG dental group with $34m profit guarantee
data.description:
The acquisition will be funded through a mix of cash and new ordinary shares.

Q & M Dental Group (Singapore) Limited has signed a non-binding memorandum of understanding for the proposed acquisition of a Singapore dental group.

Under the proposed deal, the company intends to acquire 100% of the group’s issued share capital, according to a bourse filing.

The purchase consideration will be paid through a combination of cash and new ordinary shares issued by Q & M, which will be subject to a 15-year moratorium.

The acquisition is subject to definitive agreements being signed by 31 July 2026, due diligence on the dental group and its subsidiaries, and other conditions.

The transaction will be supported by a profit guarantee of up to $34m over five years and 15-year service agreements for the sellers.

Q & M said the acquisition is part of the company’s strategy to expand its dental business in Singapore, which remains its core market.

data.title: Early layoff notices may speed up job cuts, skip negotiations: Tan See Leng
data.description:
Tan noted businesses worry about confidential, market-sensitive info being leaked.

Mandating advance notices for retrenchments could push companies to finalise them faster, potentially discouraging negotiations, according to Minister for Manpower Tan See Leng.

“Retrenchment is often, in fact, I will say always, a difficult process for all parties involved and is often a last resort for companies,” he said during the Committee of Supply debate.

Tan also noted that businesses have expressed concerns over the risk of confidential, market-sensitive information being disclosed.

The proposal was raised by Members of Parliament Patrick Tay and Ng Chee Meng, who asked whether Mandatory Retrenchment Notifications could be brought forward.

“We are not ruling out any option. We are consulting tripartite partners on these issues, and will update in due course,” Tan added.

data.title: Substantial EnGro shareholder sees interest fall to 7.48% after structural changes
data.description:
His direct holding remains at 1.2 million shares.

Tan Chin Hoon, a substantial shareholder of EnGro Corporation Limited, saw his deemed interest fall to 21.5 million shares (7.48%) following the cessation of shareholding in New Town Development Pte Ltd on 12 December 2025.

His direct holding remains at 1.2 million shares (0.42%), whilst his deemed interest through related entities fell from 23 million shares (8.00%), according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange.

EnGro has a total of 287.61 million issued shares excluding treasury shares.

The filing was made under the Securities and Futures Act, which requires substantial shareholders to disclose any material changes in direct or deemed shareholdings in SGX-listed companies.
